{"key_event":"Reza Khan’s centralizing military state-building","reasoning":"By the late 1920s the unified army, conscription, disarmament campaigns, fiscal reforms, courts, schools, and provincial administration had transformed Tehran from a weak, contested center into a moderate-capacity state with much broader coercive and administrative reach. Civil society retained some urban, clerical, bazaar, and labor capacity, but constitutional pluralism, the press, parties, unions, tribal autonomy, and regional armed movements were increasingly subordinated or repressed.","society_change":-1.2,"society_power":4.2,"state_change":3.8,"state_power":6.2}
